title: Connection refused on default port 5678 in Docker
symptom: When trying to access n8n on localhost:5678, the connection is refused. Changing to port 7889 allows access, but OAuth2 redirect still points to 5678.
not_this_if: Accessing n8n on the correct host port (e.g., 7889)
root_cause: The default internal port for n8n is 5678, but if the host port is changed to something else (like 7889), the OAuth2 redirect URL generated by n8n still uses the default port, leading to connection issues.
fix_steps: Set the `WEBHOOK_URL` environment variable to match the host port (e.g., `http://localhost:7889`).
Restart the n8n Docker container with the updated environment variable.
agent_instruction: Update the `WEBHOOK_URL` environment variable to reflect the host port you are using.
